profile.name: Petyr Baelish
profile.currentTitle: Lord Protector of the Vale & Master of Coin
profile.location: The Eyrie, The Vale of Arryn
profile.summary: The most successful self-made operator in Westerosi history. Born to a minor lordship with zero political capital, built a financial empire through brothels, trade networks, and strategic lending — then leveraged that wealth into political power that toppled kingdoms. Master of Coin, Lord of Harrenhal, Lord Protector of the Vale. Orchestrated the War of the Five Kings from the shadows. Proved that in a world of swords, the man who holds the purse strings holds the real power.

evidence.outcome: Grew Crown revenues tenfold as Master of Coin through innovative taxation, trade policies, and lending arrangements
evidence.description: Grew Crown revenues tenfold as Master of Coin through innovative taxation, trade policies, and lending arrangements

evidence.outcome: Built personal fortune from minor lordship through brothel networks, shipping investments, and strategic lending
evidence.description: Built personal fortune from minor lordship through brothel networks, shipping investments, and strategic lending

evidence.outcome: Structured Iron Bank debt instruments that funded the Crown while creating leverage for himself
evidence.description: Structured Iron Bank debt instruments that funded the Crown while creating leverage for himself

evidence.outcome: Orchestrated the murder of Jon Arryn — the event that triggered the entire War of the Five Kings
evidence.description: Orchestrated the murder of Jon Arryn — the event that triggered the entire War of the Five Kings

evidence.outcome: Manipulated Ned Stark into confronting Cersei and then betrayed him — removing the most dangerous threat to his plans
evidence.description: Manipulated Ned Stark into confronting Cersei and then betrayed him — removing the most dangerous threat to his plans

evidence.outcome: Convinced Lysa Arryn to poison her husband, frame the Lannisters, and draw Ned Stark to King's Landing — a single scheme that reshaped the continent
evidence.description: Convinced Lysa Arryn to poison her husband, frame the Lannisters, and draw Ned Stark to King's Landing — a single scheme that reshaped the continent

evidence.outcome: Maintained the appearance of a loyal servant to every faction simultaneously — Crown, Stark, Lannister, Tyrell, Arryn
evidence.description: Maintained the appearance of a loyal servant to every faction simultaneously — Crown, Stark, Lannister, Tyrell, Arryn

evidence.outcome: Created chaos between major houses while positioning himself as indispensable mediator to all sides
evidence.description: Created chaos between major houses while positioning himself as indispensable mediator to all sides

evidence.outcome: His true allegiances were unknown to every major player in Westeros until it was too late
evidence.description: His true allegiances were unknown to every major player in Westeros until it was too late

evidence.outcome: Co-engineered assassination of King Joffrey at the Purple Wedding without any evidence linking back to him
evidence.description: Coordinated with Olenna Tyrell to poison Joffrey during his own wedding feast, using Sansa's hairnet as the delivery mechanism while maintaining complete deniability

evidence.outcome: Built the most comprehensive intelligence network in King's Landing through his brothel operations
evidence.description: Built the most comprehensive intelligence network in King's Landing through his brothel operations

evidence.outcome: Gathered compromising information on lords, ladies, and merchants — using it for leverage rather than exposure
evidence.description: Gathered compromising information on lords, ladies, and merchants — using it for leverage rather than exposure

evidence.outcome: Controlled the flow of information between factions — deciding who knew what and when
evidence.description: Controlled the flow of information between factions — deciding who knew what and when

evidence.outcome: Orchestrated the arrest of Ned Stark by feeding false assurances of City Watch loyalty while secretly securing their allegiance to the Lannisters
evidence.description: Promised Ned the City Watch would support his claim as regent, then betrayed him at the critical moment, demonstrating control over institutional power through information manipulation

evidence.outcome: Created multiple revenue streams including brothels, customs collection, trade facilitation, and money lending
evidence.description: Created multiple revenue streams including brothels, customs collection, trade facilitation, and money lending

evidence.outcome: Designed taxation schemes that maximized Crown revenue while building personal wealth simultaneously
evidence.description: Designed taxation schemes that maximized Crown revenue while building personal wealth simultaneously

evidence.outcome: Monetized information itself — selling intelligence, introductions, and favors as tradeable commodities
evidence.description: Monetized information itself — selling intelligence, introductions, and favors as tradeable commodities

evidence.outcome: Acquired the title Lord of Harrenhal — the largest castle in Westeros — through pure political maneuvering
evidence.description: Acquired the title Lord of Harrenhal — the largest castle in Westeros — through pure political maneuvering

evidence.outcome: Gained control of the Vale of Arryn through marriage to Lysa and subsequent guardianship of Robin Arryn
evidence.description: Gained control of the Vale of Arryn through marriage to Lysa and subsequent guardianship of Robin Arryn

evidence.outcome: Built a portfolio of political, financial, and real estate assets from essentially nothing
evidence.description: Built a portfolio of political, financial, and real estate assets from essentially nothing

evidence.outcome: Eliminated Lysa Arryn to become sole regent of the Vale, controlling the largest uncommitted army in Westeros
evidence.description: Pushed Lysa through the Moon Door after she threatened Sansa, then convinced witnesses it was suicide, securing control of the Eyrie and its military forces

evidence.outcome: Cultivated relationships across every social stratum — from smallfolk informants to the King himself
evidence.description: Cultivated relationships across every social stratum — from smallfolk informants to the King himself

evidence.outcome: Built a network of allies, debtors, and dependents that spanned the Seven Kingdoms
evidence.description: Built a network of allies, debtors, and dependents that spanned the Seven Kingdoms

evidence.outcome: Used personal relationships as investment vehicles — each friendship was a strategic position
evidence.description: Used personal relationships as investment vehicles — each friendship was a strategic position

evidence.outcome: Brokered the Tyrell-Lannister alliance that won the Battle of the Blackwater and saved King's Landing
evidence.description: Connected the Tyrells' military strength with the Lannisters' political authority, engineering the partnership that decided the war while positioning himself as indispensable to both sides

evidence.outcome: Executed a multi-decade strategy to rise from minor lord to potential ruler of the Seven Kingdoms
evidence.description: Executed a multi-decade strategy to rise from minor lord to potential ruler of the Seven Kingdoms

evidence.outcome: Positioned chess pieces over years — the Stark-Lannister conflict, the Tyrell alliance, the Vale control — before making any visible move
evidence.description: Positioned chess pieces over years — the Stark-Lannister conflict, the Tyrell alliance, the Vale control — before making any visible move

evidence.outcome: Planned the assassination of Joffrey in collaboration with the Tyrells — eliminating an unstable king while framing Tyrion
evidence.description: Planned the assassination of Joffrey in collaboration with the Tyrells — eliminating an unstable king while framing Tyrion

evidence.outcome: Maintained plausible deniability for every major action — never directly connected to his schemes
evidence.description: Maintained plausible deniability for every major action — never directly connected to his schemes

evidence.outcome: Diversified his power base across multiple regions and factions to survive any single alliance collapse
evidence.description: Diversified his power base across multiple regions and factions to survive any single alliance collapse

evidence.outcome: Used intermediaries and cutouts for sensitive operations — keeping his hands clean while others took the risks
evidence.description: Used intermediaries and cutouts for sensitive operations — keeping his hands clean while others took the risks

evidence.outcome: Convinced Sansa Stark to trust him despite his betrayal of her father
evidence.description: Convinced Sansa Stark to trust him despite his betrayal of her father

evidence.outcome: Persuaded the Lords of the Vale to march North — positioning himself as savior of the Battle of the Bastards
evidence.description: Persuaded the Lords of the Vale to march North — positioning himself as savior of the Battle of the Bastards

evidence.outcome: Sold every faction on a different version of his loyalty — each believing they had his true allegiance
evidence.description: Sold every faction on a different version of his loyalty — each believing they had his true allegiance

evidence.outcome: Identified opportunities in Westerosi markets that others overlooked — particularly the intersection of vice and intelligence
evidence.description: Identified opportunities in Westerosi markets that others overlooked — particularly the intersection of vice and intelligence

evidence.outcome: Understood that political instability creates financial opportunity — chaos is a ladder
evidence.description: Understood that political instability creates financial opportunity — chaos is a ladder

evidence.outcome: Analyzed the weaknesses of every major house's financial position and exploited them through lending and trade manipulation
evidence.description: Analyzed the weaknesses of every major house's financial position and exploited them through lending and trade manipulation

evidence.outcome: Possessed encyclopedic knowledge of Crown finances, noble debts, and trade relationships accumulated over 12 years as Master of Coin
evidence.description: Possessed encyclopedic knowledge of Crown finances, noble debts, and trade relationships accumulated over 12 years as Master of Coin

evidence.outcome: Understood the bureaucratic machinery of the Iron Throne better than anyone except perhaps Varys
evidence.description: Understood the bureaucratic machinery of the Iron Throne better than anyone except perhaps Varys

evidence.outcome: Used institutional knowledge as leverage — knowing where the financial bodies were buried
evidence.description: Used institutional knowledge as leverage — knowing where the financial bodies were buried

evidence.outcome: Identified and exploited the specific psychological vulnerabilities of every target — Ned's honor, Cersei's paranoia, Lysa's obsession
evidence.description: Identified and exploited the specific psychological vulnerabilities of every target — Ned's honor, Cersei's paranoia, Lysa's obsession

evidence.outcome: Recruited operatives based on their weaknesses rather than their strengths — ensuring loyalty through dependency
evidence.description: Recruited operatives based on their weaknesses rather than their strengths — ensuring loyalty through dependency

evidence.outcome: Used Ros, Dontos, and other agents as disposable assets in larger strategic operations
evidence.description: Used Ros, Dontos, and other agents as disposable assets in larger strategic operations

evidence.outcome: Turned every crisis into a personal advancement opportunity — the War of the Five Kings was essentially his creation and his harvest
evidence.description: Turned every crisis into a personal advancement opportunity — the War of the Five Kings was essentially his creation and his harvest

evidence.outcome: Used the aftermath of Joffrey's assassination to remove Sansa from King's Landing — acquiring his most valuable long-term asset
evidence.description: Used the aftermath of Joffrey's assassination to remove Sansa from King's Landing — acquiring his most valuable long-term asset

evidence.outcome: Exploited the Bolton occupation of Winterfell to position himself as liberator of the North
evidence.description: Exploited the Bolton occupation of Winterfell to position himself as liberator of the North

evidence.outcome: Conducted thorough assessment of every political and financial opportunity before committing
evidence.description: Conducted thorough assessment of every political and financial opportunity before committing

evidence.outcome: Researched the backgrounds, debts, and secrets of every significant figure in King's Landing
evidence.description: Researched the backgrounds, debts, and secrets of every significant figure in King's Landing

evidence.outcome: Maintained detailed mental accounting of favors owed, debts outstanding, and leverage available
evidence.description: Maintained detailed mental accounting of favors owed, debts outstanding, and leverage available

evidence.outcome: Maintained information compartmentalization across his entire network — no single agent knew the full picture
evidence.description: Maintained information compartmentalization across his entire network — no single agent knew the full picture

evidence.outcome: Used secure communication channels and trusted intermediaries for sensitive operations
evidence.description: Used secure communication channels and trusted intermediaries for sensitive operations

evidence.outcome: Destroyed evidence and eliminated witnesses when necessary to protect operational integrity
evidence.description: Destroyed evidence and eliminated witnesses when necessary to protect operational integrity

evidence.outcome: Rose from Lord of the smallest finger of land in the Vale to Lord Protector controlling one of the Seven Kingdoms
evidence.description: Rose from Lord of the smallest finger of land in the Vale to Lord Protector controlling one of the Seven Kingdoms

evidence.outcome: Navigated aristocratic society despite being openly mocked for his lowborn origins
evidence.description: Navigated aristocratic society despite being openly mocked for his lowborn origins

evidence.outcome: Used each new title as a platform for the next acquisition — customs officer to Master of Coin to Lord of Harrenhal to Lord Protector
evidence.description: Used each new title as a platform for the next acquisition — customs officer to Master of Coin to Lord of Harrenhal to Lord Protector

purpose: Chaos isn't a pit. Chaos is a ladder. The climb is all there is — and every rung is someone else's ceiling.

workHistory.title: Lord Protector of the Vale
workHistory.company: The Vale of Arryn — The Eyrie
workHistory.description: Ruled the Vale as guardian of Robin Arryn. Commanded the Knights of the Vale — the largest untouched army in Westeros. Leveraged military power to influence the Northern succession.

workHistory.title: Master of Coin
workHistory.description: Chief financial officer of the Seven Kingdoms for 12 years. Grew Crown revenues tenfold. Built personal fortune and intelligence network simultaneously. Served under two kings.

workHistory.title: Revenue Commissioner
workHistory.description: Managed customs and revenue collection. Demonstrated exceptional financial acumen that caught the attention of Jon Arryn, leading to appointment to the Small Council.

workHistory.title: Customs Officer
workHistory.description: Entry-level government position. Began building financial expertise and political connections in the Vale's primary trade port. First step on the ladder.
